title: Elegy For A Jet Pilot
content:
The blast skims
over the string
of takeoff lights
and
relinquishing
place and time
lofts to
separation:
the plume, rose
sliver, grows
across the
high-lit evening
sky: by this
Mays Landing creek
shot pinecones,
skinned huckleberry
bush, laurel
swaths define
an unbelievably
particular stop.
meaning:
A. R. Ammons's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Elegy For A Jet Pilot"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
